summary: A Kaggle dataset containing a sample of Elon Musk's tweets. The extracted tweets highlight his critical stance on modern journalism and his personal, humorous variation of Occam's Razor.

principles.statement: Most modern journalism is unoriginal and lacks integrity.
principles.rationale: He observes that reporters often just repackage existing internet content rather than conducting genuine investigative work.
principles.source_quote: 99% of journalism is reading someone else's internet and printing it on paper

mental_models.name: Musk's Occam's Razor
mental_models.description: An ironic twist on the philosophical principle of Occam's Razor, positing that the most entertaining or ironic outcome is the one that will most likely happen.

heuristics: Assume the most entertaining outcome is the most likely.

signature_quotes.text: My version of Occam’s Razor: most entertaining outcome is most likely …
99% of journalism is reading someone else's internet and printing it on paper

anti_patterns.description: Relying on mainstream journalism for original truth.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Because the vast majority of it is just reading what is already on the internet and printing it on paper, often lacking true journalistic integrity.
